title: AI for Good Global Summit 2026 + UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance
host: ITU + UN system + Government of Switzerland
why: The strongest multilateral fixture in the AI governance calendar, combining policy, standards, capacity-building and practical deployment signal.
watch: Prioritise if you care about foreign government positioning, UN-system architecture, and emerging global governance norms.

title: CPDP 2026 · Competing Visions, Shared Futures
angle: European digital governance / AI Act / enforcement
why: High-signal Brussels conference covering AI governance, digital rulemaking, privacy, sovereignty and resilience.
watch: Useful for EU AI Act implementation, digital policy interaction, and practical enforcement debates.

title: IEEE Symposium on Security & Privacy 2026
why: Premier academic venue for offensive and defensive security research, with a growing track on adversarial ML, model theft, and AI-enabled attacks.
watch: Watch for papers on LLM exploitation, AI-assisted vulnerability discovery, and autonomous attack tooling.

title: Global Conference on Criminal Justice and AI
why: Focuses on AI as both a tool for law enforcement and a vector for organised crime, terrorism financing, and judicial bias.
watch: Key for anyone tracking AI in prosecutorial decision-making, predictive policing controversy, and cross-border evidence rules.

title: NeurIPS 2026 — Workshop on Socially Responsible ML
why: The largest ML conference hosts a dedicated workshop on fairness, accountability, robustness, and societal harms from deployed models.
watch: Scan for papers on dual-use model capabilities, safety benchmarks, and red-teaming methodologies.

title: Internet Governance Forum 2026
why: Annual UN multistakeholder forum; 2026 will centre AI governance, platform liability, and cross-border data flows.
watch: Watch for emerging-economy positions on AI regulation and the GDC follow-up process.

title: Europol Cybercrime Conference 2026
why: Law-enforcement-facing conference covering AI-enabled fraud, deepfake crime, ransomware-as-a-service, and crypto asset tracing.
watch: High signal for operational AI misuse trends — attacker TTPs that feed directly into defensive policy.

title: Biosecurity and Emerging Technology Summit
host: Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security
why: Examines AI-accelerated dual-use biology risks, including protein-folding tools, automated lab systems, and biosurveillance.
watch: Critical for understanding where frontier AI intersects with biological weapons risk and pandemic preparedness gaps.

title: Electoral Integrity & AI Disinformation Forum
why: Roundtable on AI-generated influence operations, synthetic media in elections, and platform regulatory responses.
watch: Monitor for voluntary code commitments from platforms and emerging legal frameworks for synthetic electoral content.

title: UN Group of Governmental Experts on LAWS
why: Intergovernmental expert group negotiating norms around lethal autonomous weapons systems under the Convention on Certain Conventional Weapons.
watch: The clearest signal on where states stand on meaningful human control and autonomous targeting — directly shapes future treaty language.
